architecture_layers.value: Three read-only layers, imports flow downward only: data/ (committed sample snapshot JSON — the only data source in stage 1) -> server/ (stdlib http.server backend: GET /api/snapshot + static file serving) -> web/ (static frontend that talks to the backend ONLY via the JSON API). The frontend never reads data/ directly; the server never writes; no database, no auth, no secrets anywhere in this repo.

drift_resolution.value: Source code wins over docs: when a doc contradicts merged code, fix the doc in the same session, note the correction in docs/current-state.md

fleet_dark_repos.value: All fleet siblings are public and readable EXCEPT `pokemon-mod-lab` (private — raw-content fetches 404; never read it, never guess about it). Standing read authorization: superbot Q-0272.

fleet_siblings.value: Canonical roster (~19 repos): superbot `docs/fleet-reading-path.md` (repo map + reading tiers) and substrate-kit `docs/adopters.md` (per-adopter kit versions). Key rows — superbot: hub/production bot, truth: `docs/current-state.md` + `docs/fleet-reading-path.md` · fleet-manager: manager seat, truth: `control/status.md` + inbox ORDERs · substrate-kit: workflow kit, truth: `docs/adopters.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` · superbot-next: rebuild target · websites · superbot-games · trading-strategy · gba-homebrew · venture-lab · idea-engine · superbot-idle · superbot-mineverse (this repo): each carries its truth in `control/status.md` (heartbeat) + `docs/current-state.md` · pokemon-mod-lab: DARK (private, unreadable).

fleet_status_command.value: Clone the hub repo `menno420/superbot` and run `python3.10 scripts/fleet_status.py` — per-seat heartbeat table + manager roster/owner-queue. Reading route: superbot `docs/fleet-reading-path.md` (canonical fleet map).

mutation_seam.value: Stage 1 is read-only end to end: the web app serves a committed snapshot and has no write path. All repo mutations go branch -> PR -> substrate-gate green -> squash merge; direct pushes to main are blocked

new_area_ownership.value: New top-level areas default to this Project (agent sessions) and land via reviewed PRs; the manager owns control/inbox.md exclusively

owner_profile.value: Menno (menno420) — solo owner of the superbot fleet; steers agent sessions through control/ orders and reviews shipped PRs asynchronously; prefers small, tested, autonomous PRs with plain-language before/after reports

ownership_model.value: Single owner (menno420); agent sessions write only via PRs gated on substrate-gate; one writer per control file (manager: control/inbox.md, this Project: control/status.md)

primary_language.value: Python 3.10 (stdlib-only backend) plus a vanilla HTML/JS/CSS frontend with no build step

review_ritual.value: Every change ships branch -> PR -> substrate-gate green -> squash merge; the owner reviews merged PRs asynchronously and docs/current-state.md is updated with each shipped PR

staleness_review.value: Docs untouched past the kit staleness window (14 days) get re-verified against live source by the next session that reads them
